vulnerabilities.description: stdouttrace exporter may expose sensitive data (tokens, credentials, user data) in logs in production. Default stdout tracing with pretty print logs all request/response data.
vulnerabilities.fix: Remove stdout exporter in production. Use OTLP or Jaeger with proper sampling and sensitive data filtering. Add config check to disable tracing in production.

vulnerabilities.description: State parameter comparison may be vulnerable to timing attacks. Using simple string comparison for OAuth state allows timing side-channel attacks.
vulnerabilities.fix: Use crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare() for state parameter validation to prevent timing attacks.

vulnerabilities.description: Incomplete SAML implementation visible. No evidence of XML Signature validation, assertion encryption, or replay attack protection (NotOnOrAfter Before conditions).
vulnerabilities.fix: Implement full XML signature validation with IdP certificate, check assertion conditions (NotBefore/NotOnOrAfter), validate audience restriction, implement replay protection with unique ID tracking.

vulnerabilities.description: Claims structure includes sensitive information (email, org_id, role) in JWT. If tokens are logged or leaked, PII is exposed. No evidence of audience (aud) or issuer (iss) claim validation in ValidateToken.
vulnerabilities.fix: Add audience and issuer claim validation. Minimize sensitive data in access tokens - use opaque reference tokens or store minimal claims. Implement token fingerprinting.

vulnerabilities.description: OAuth state stored only in memory (map with mutex). Not scalable across multiple service instances. State can be replayed within expiration window. No cleanup of expired states.
vulnerabilities.fix: Use Redis or similar distributed cache for state storage. Implement state expiration and cleanup. Add PKCE (RFC 7636) for additional security.

vulnerabilities.description: Pagination validation does not protect against excessive resource consumption. Very large limit values (up to maxLimit) could be abused.
vulnerabilities.fix: Add reasonable default maxLimit (e.g., 100). Consider rate limiting per user/IP. Monitor for abuse patterns.

vulnerabilities.description: No evidence of key rotation mechanism. JWT secrets must be rotated periodically for security.
vulnerabilities.fix: Implement key rotation support with kid (key ID) header. Maintain multiple valid keys during rotation period.

owasp_checks.detail: JWT validation present, middleware structure exists for auth
Argon2id used for passwords with proper parameters (OWASP compliant)
No SQL injection patterns found in provided code. Uses parameterized patterns.
Missing rate limiting, state management not distributed, no replay protection evident in SAML
stdout tracer may leak sensitive data in production. No evidence of security headers.
Good password hashing. Timing attack vulnerability in OAuth state. Missing key rotation.
SAML signature validation not visible. No evidence of subresource integrity checks.
No SSRF patterns found in provided code

critical_fixes: 1. Remove/fix stdouttrace exporter to prevent sensitive data logging in production
2. Fix OAuth state timing attack vulnerability using ConstantTimeCompare
3. Implement complete SAML signature and assertion validation
4. Add JWT audience and issuer claim validation
5. Implement distributed state storage (Redis) for OAuth flow scalability
